repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
## Problem
stdpath() may return a DOS 8.3 "shortened" filename, because Windows truncates long usernames into `6ch~N` names in `TEMP/TMP` env vars. We don't want to "leak" them into Nvim.

## Solution
For "run", pass `true` to `vim_FullName` to expand 8.3 filenames. For "cache", call `os_realpath` to expand 8.3 filenames.

<!--
  Thank you for contributing to Neovim!
  If this is your first time, check out https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#pull-requests-prs
  for our PR guidelines.
-->

fix #25019

comment_data.body:
```suggestion
  it('avoids DOS 8.3 filenames for "cache" and "run" #25019', function()
```

could the `clear()` above this be dropped ? can just use the test runner's `vim.fn.system` ?

```suggestion
    local short_path = vim.fn.system(('for %%I in ("%s") do @echo %%~sI'):format(dir), '')
      :gsub('\n', '')
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1282,4 +1282,26 @@ describe('stdpath()', function()
       })
     end)
   end)
+
+  it('avoids DOS 8.3 filenames for "cache" and "run" #25019', function()
+    t.skip(not is_os('win'), 'N/A: 8.3 filenames are only available on Windows')
+    local dir = 'Xtest_temp_path'
+    mkdir(dir)
+    finally(function()
+      rmdir(dir)
+    end)
+    clear()
+    local short_path = fn.system(('for %%I in ("%s") do @echo %%~sI'):format(dir), '')
+      :gsub('\n', '')

comment_data.body:
to make the test extra clear and readable, since 8.3 names should be "predictable", can we hardcode an assert?

    eq('...', short_path)
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1282,4 +1282,26 @@ describe('stdpath()', function()
       })
     end)
   end)
+
+  it('avoids DOS 8.3 filenames for "cache" and "run" #25019', function()
+    t.skip(not is_os('win'), 'N/A: 8.3 filenames are only available on Windows')
+    local dir = 'Xtest_temp_path'
+    mkdir(dir)
+    finally(function()
+      rmdir(dir)
+    end)
+    clear()
+    local short_path = fn.system(('for %%I in ("%s") do @echo %%~sI'):format(dir), '')
+      :gsub('\n', '')
+    t.skip(dir == vim.fs.basename(short_path), 'N/A: 8.3 filenames are disabled')

comment_data.body: Wow, it works—I wasn't aware this was possible.

comment_data.body:
replace `t.skip` with something like `eq('XTEST_~1', vim.fs.basename(short_path))`?
I can try that. I originally chosen that way for 2 reasons:
* I couldn't find the exact rules for 8dot3name generation. Here it's likely `XTEST_~1`, but I've also seen some hash-like names in other dirs.
* Using `skip` ensures that if someone disables 8dot3name, the test will be reported as skipped rather than failed.

comment_data.body:
> something like `eq('XTEST_~1', vim.fs.basename(short_path))`?

yes. should be stable i think?



> if someone disables 8dot3name, the test will be reported as skipped

is that a system-level setting? i don't think we need to worry about that. or rather, it's a tradeoff because if CI starts disabling that, we're unlikely to notice if it's "skipped"...

@@ -1282,4 +1282,26 @@ describe('stdpath()', function()

+    t.skip(dir == vim.fs.basename(short_path), 'N/A: 8.3 filenames are disabled')

comment_data.body: > yes. should be stable i think? Eh, should be stable, GPT tells me the hash-like names in my `Shared Folders` are generated by `VMware`. For normal Volumes, Windows typically uses traditional `XTEST_~1` style, and switches to hash when there are 5+ similar names. > is that a system-level setting? i don't think we need to worry about that. or rather, it's a tradeoff because if CI starts disabling that, we're unlikely to notice if it's "skipped"... It has both a global state and volume state, the setting can be changed via `fsutil.exe`

comment_regular_data.body: hmm... Windows CI has timed out twice in a row.

> hmm... Windows CI has timed out twice in a row.

Failed now, CI disabled 8dot3name? Let me check the prev logs. 😅
```shell
FAILED   ...st_xdg_options/test/functional/options/defaults_spec.lua @ 1286: stdpath() avoids DOS 8.3 filenames for "cache" and "run" #25019
Expected values to be equal.
Expected:
"XTEST_~1"
Actual:
"Xtest_temp_path"
```

Looks like it's disabled.
```shell
RUN      T76 stdpath() returns a List with "data_dirs" doesn't expand ~/ set at runtime: 58.96 ms OK
RUN      T77 stdpath() avoids DOS 8.3 filenames for "cache" and "run" #25019: 77.54 ms SKIP
-------- 60 tests from test/functional/options/defaults_spec.lua (5302.82 ms total)
```

ref: https://github.com/actions/runner-images/pull/9862
Tests pass after enabling 8dot3name via `fsutil`. Do we need to disable it again after the test run? It doesn't seem to have much performance impact. 😂

title: fix(stdpath): avoid DOS 8.3 filenames for "cache" and "run"
